behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the behavior is not only JSON parsing; it must also "proceed through normal hook input handling," which is a parser->handler module handoff. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess is required to reproduce this failure; an in-process harness can feed payload strings and verify downstream handling. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no AI output dependency or agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process test that sends multiple valid JSON payloads whose string fields include code fences, backticks, and `$vars`; assert parse succeeds and the next hook-input handler receives the expected structured object without entering error/trace-failure path.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at parser-error -> trace-record wiring boundary (multiple local modules). REAL-INFRA: behavior text requires malformed JSON handling and trace content, not real process or network effects. REJECTION-GATE for System: behavior says "produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record" but does not require real stdin/process lifecycle; in-process injection is sufficient. No LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process malformed payload corpus (e.g., truncated object, unclosed quote, bad comma) and assert trace sink receives `json_parse_failed` plus diagnostic context fields (offset/snippet/error message key) that identify malformed region.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly says "local error-policy path" and checks mapping + telemetry intent, which is cross-module policy handling rather than pure parser logic. REAL-INFRA brake: no OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: In-process test forces a parse-error object into the error-policy entrypoint; assert returned result is handled (no exception), status is non-throwing, and telemetry-intent marker/flag is set for later emission.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly requires "real process execution" and malformed stdin lifecycle (stdin, process stability, exit ordering). MOCK-HIDE: in-process mocks can miss crash/exit-code/flush timing issues. No LLM dependency, no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the hook as a subprocess, pipe malformed JSON to stdin, wait for exit, and assert: process does not crash unexpectedly, expected `json_parse_failed` trace signal is emitted before termination, and exit behavior matches policy.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: "real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink)" implies exercising actual I/O path and artifact emission end-to-end. MOCK-HIDE: faking stdin or sink can hide integration failures in buffering, wiring, or artifact creation timing. No LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the hook through its real entrypoint with malformed stdin and assert the real trace artifact (e.g., emitted record/file/log event) exists and contains `json_parse_failed` for that invocation.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is a classification/regression boundary across parse outcome + failure attribution paths, usually parser + trace/error categorization modules. REAL-INFRA brake: no required OS/network/subprocess in behavior text. REJECTION-GATE for System: behavior focuses on distinction logic, not process lifecycle. No LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run paired in-process cases: (A) valid JSON with markdown-heavy strings and (B) malformed JSON; assert only (B) triggers `json_parse_failed`, while (A) follows normal path, with explicit assertion that markdown tokens alone never set parse-failure attribution.
